pattern: Reading the sorted list into order and taking each slice's middle as a root turns the chain into a height-balanced BST whose in-order reading is the original list.
hints: A balanced tree needs each root to roughly halve what is left, so the middle element of the current run is the root.
Walk the list once into an indexable form, then recurse: middle becomes the node, the part before it becomes the left subtree, the part after it the right.
Any consistent middle (e.g. the lower middle of lo..hi) gives a valid height-balanced BST; the in-order traversal is just the list in order.

edge_inputs.description: empty list yields an empty tree
even length so the middle is ambiguous
power-of-two-minus-one length builds a perfect tree

stress.description: long sorted list of distinct values
stress.note: ascending sorted as the problem requires
